ui.sub: There are 8,200,000,000 people on this planet. 1 dollar won't kill anyone. It will make me a billionaire. You lose nothing, I gain absolutely everything.
ui.legal: It's a gift. No refunds. I'm 18+. Read the terms.
ui.sSub: Somewhere in Central Europe a tired guy just looked at his phone and smiled. That was me. Because of you.
ui.ap2: I'm an ordinary guy from Central Europe. I get up at five, go to the factory, come back tired. Over and over.
ui.ap3: I'm not a company. I'm not a charity. I'm not an influencer. No sob story. I'm not pretending to save the world.
ui.ap4: One idea wouldn't leave my head: what if I actually tried? If everyone gave me a dollar, nobody would even notice. I'd be a billionaire. So here I am.
ui.ap5: No promises. No rewards. No NFTs. Just a cash gift and a thank-you. If it works — I'll never have to set an alarm again. If it doesn't — at least I tried!
ui.ft: © onedollarguy.com · Not a charity · Personal gifts only
ui.kontakt_msg: I want to stay anonymous — but if you still want to contact me, here's my email:
ui.share_tweet: I just gave a dollar to One Dollar Guy. 8.2 billion to go. {url} Come join in!
ui.legal_error: Could not load the document. Please try again.

faq: If I were a scammer, I'd have come up with a better story. Saving the world, an AI startup… But no — I'm telling you straight: I want money, and I'm not pretending it's for anything else. I'm honest. Payments go through global payment gateways — everything is secure.
Why should I even give you a dollar?

You don't have to. Nobody's forcing you. But think about it — when was the last time you did something that cost you practically nothing, but might have given someone hope? One "dollar" — ideally more 😄. It's up to you. All I can do is thank you.
What will you do with the money?

It won't be that hot 😄 From one dollar, after everything, barely anything is left for me.  I want to travel. I've never been on a plane. I want to eat good food. Not to worry whether I'll make it through the month. I won't pay off my loan. And one day I might buy an old American car — that's been a dream since I was a kid. I know that if the project works out, I'd definitely help others too. And if it goes extremely well? As the classic said — hookers, booze and canapés 😄.

Honza. 30, my hair's already thinning 😄 I work with my hands. Since I was a kid I've fixed everything myself — what I couldn't fix, I didn't have. I like working out and eating good food. I'm an introvert, I love animals and nature. I've never had anything for free, but they say miracles happen, so we'll see.
Do you really think this will work?
I don't know. But I know one thing — if I didn't try, I'd know for sure it wouldn't. The biggest regrets in life aren't the things that didn't work out. They're the ones we never even started.
